blurb: Provides legal services to government, oversees the justice system and courts administration, and protects rights through legal aid, human rights, and family justice services.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: B.C.'s courts are fair, safe and accessible
goals.description: The ministry administers court registries, sheriff services and court processes across 90 court locations, and helps people resolve civil, small claims and traffic matters.

main_services.name: Resolve a civil or small claims dispute

supporting.name: Resolve a small claims dispute
supporting.desc: Small Claims Court settles disputes worth up to $35,000, with simpler and less formal rules and procedures than Supreme Court. Most people are encouraged to settle by agreement before a trial is needed.
supporting.users: People and businesses with a civil dispute up to $35,000

legislation.full_name: Small Claims Act  -  Small Claims Court procedures and monetary jurisdiction

supporting.name: Resolve a low-value dispute through the Civil Resolution Tribunal
supporting.desc: The Civil Resolution Tribunal resolves most small claims disputes up to $5,000, along with strata property, societies and cooperative association disputes, mostly online.
supporting.users: People and businesses with a low-value civil, strata or society dispute

legislation.full_name: Civil Resolution Tribunal Act  -  Tribunal jurisdiction over small claims, strata and society disputes

supporting.name: Pay or dispute a traffic ticket
supporting.desc: Provincial violation tickets and federal contravention tickets can be paid online through PayBC, or disputed at a court registry.
supporting.users: Drivers, vehicle owners

supporting.name: File court documents and get court records or transcripts
supporting.desc: Court documents and forms can be filed in person, by mail, fax, or electronically through Court Services Online. People can also request court file information and transcripts.
supporting.users: Litigants, legal professionals, members of the public

supporting.name: Find a court location or book a virtual registry appointment
supporting.desc: There are 90 court locations across B.C., 43 with registries. A virtual counter lets people book an appointment to speak with a court registry employee by phone or video.
supporting.users: Members of the public, litigants

supporting.name: Find daily court schedules and case lists
supporting.desc: Daily court lists show the location and time of civil, family and criminal court proceedings happening across the province, including completed case results.
supporting.users: Members of the public, litigants, media

supporting.name: Serve on a jury
supporting.desc: A jury is a group of people selected to hear a criminal or civil trial, or a Coroner's inquest, and reach a decision on the case.
supporting.users: Members of the public called for jury duty

supporting.name: Get a court interpreter
supporting.desc: Interpreters help people who have difficulty understanding or speaking English, or who are deaf or hard of hearing, understand and participate in court proceedings.
supporting.users: Litigants, witnesses, newcomers, deaf and hard of hearing people

main_services.name: Get help filling out and filing court forms

supporting.name: Apply for a Family Law Act order online
supporting.desc: An online service helps people fill out Provincial Court family forms to apply for a Family Law Act order, with video tutorials on how it works.
supporting.users: Separating or divorcing parents

channels.label: Apply for a family court order

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Provincial Court Family Law Act orders

supporting.name: Apply for a joint, uncontested divorce online
supporting.desc: Couples who agree on all family law issues, such as property division and support, can use the Online Divorce Assistant to jointly apply for a simple, uncontested divorce.
supporting.users: Separating couples who agree on all issues

supporting.name: Get help completing small claims forms online
supporting.desc: The Filing Assistant takes information entered online and generates completed Small Claims Court forms, developed by the ministry and the Provincial Court of B.C.
supporting.users: People filing or responding to a small claim

channels.label: Use the small claims Filing Assistant

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Families can resolve family law matters and keep children safe
goals.description: The ministry's Family Justice Services Division helps British Columbians resolve separation, divorce and parenting disputes, and enforces child and spousal support.

main_services.name: Resolve a separation, divorce or parenting dispute

supporting.name: Get in-person, phone or video help with a family law issue
supporting.desc: Family Justice Centres and Justice Access Centres offer free help with parenting arrangements, guardianship, child and spousal support, contact with a child, and property division involving companion animals.
supporting.users: Separating or divorcing families

channels.label: Family Justice Centre and Justice Access Centre locations

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Resolution of family law disputes

supporting.name: Take a Parenting After Separation course
supporting.desc: A free course helps separating or divorcing parents understand how separation affects children and how to focus on their children's needs.
supporting.users: Separating or divorcing parents

supporting.name: Resolve a family dispute through mediation
supporting.desc: Justice Access Centre staff can refer families to mediation and other dispute resolution options as an alternative to going to court.
supporting.users: Separating or divorcing families

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Out-of-court dispute resolution

supporting.name: Get help keeping your family safe from violence
supporting.desc: Justice Access Centre staff can refer people to safety planning, VictimLinkBC and protection orders for family violence, including help registering a protection order.
supporting.users: People experiencing family violence

channels.label: Keeping yourself and your family safe

supporting.name: Get help if your child has been taken to another country without permission
supporting.desc: B.C.'s central authority for international child abduction helps parents when a child has been taken to, or wrongfully retained in, another country without permission.
supporting.users: Parents and guardians

main_services.name: Get child or spousal support calculated and enforced

supporting.name: Calculate the amount of child support owed
supporting.desc: The federal child support table look-up helps parents find the base amount of child support owed based on income and number of children.
supporting.users: Separating or divorced parents

supporting.name: Enrol a support order or agreement for enforcement
supporting.desc: The BC Family Maintenance Agency collects and forwards support payments owed under a court order or agreement, calculates arrears, and takes collection action against people who don't pay.
supporting.users: People owed or paying child or spousal support

legislation.full_name: Family Maintenance Enforcement Act  -  Enforcement of support orders and agreements

supporting.name: Recalculate child support each year without going to court
supporting.desc: The Child Support Recalculation Service updates the amount of child support owed each year, based on current income, without requiring a court application.
supporting.users: Parents with a child support order

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: People are protected and supported through the criminal justice system
goals.description: The BC Prosecution Service conducts fair, independent prosecutions, and victim services help people cope with the emotional, physical and financial effects of crime.

main_services.name: Get support as a victim or witness of crime

supporting.name: Get 24-hour crisis support and referrals after a crime
supporting.desc: VictimLinkBC is a confidential, multilingual phone service available 24 hours a day that refers victims of crime and family or sexual violence to help in their community.
supporting.users: Victims of crime, family members, witnesses

supporting.name: Access victim services in your community
supporting.desc: Victim services help anyone who has been a victim of crime, their family members, and witnesses understand their options and get emotional and practical support.
supporting.users: Victims of crime, family members, witnesses

supporting.name: Apply for financial assistance as a victim of violent crime
supporting.desc: The Crime Victim Assistance Program helps victims, immediate family members and some witnesses cope with the financial effects of violent crime.
supporting.users: Victims of violent crime and their immediate family

supporting.name: Get notified about an offender's custody or release status
supporting.desc: The Victim Safety Unit provides eligible victims with information about the accused or offender, such as release dates and conditions.
supporting.users: Victims of crime

supporting.name: Get help with travel costs to attend a justice proceeding
supporting.desc: The Victim Travel Fund provides funds so a victim or family member can attend a justice-related proceeding in B.C.
supporting.users: Victims of crime and family members

supporting.name: Register a protection order
supporting.desc: The Protection Order Registry is a database of civil and criminal protection orders that helps reduce violence against vulnerable people.
supporting.users: People with a protection order, police

legislation.full_name: Family Law Act  -  Registration of protection orders

supporting.name: Prepare a victim impact statement
supporting.desc: Victims can prepare a statement describing how a crime affected them, to be considered by the court at sentencing, with a guide available from the BC Prosecution Service.
supporting.users: Victims of crime

legislation.full_name: Crown Counsel Act  -  Preparing victims for court

supporting.name: Get information as a witness in a criminal case
supporting.desc: Witnesses can find out what to expect when testifying in a criminal court case, including how Crown Counsel prepares witnesses before trial.
supporting.users: Witnesses in a criminal case

channels.label: What to Expect: Information for Witnesses

legislation.full_name: Crown Counsel Act  -  Preparing witnesses for court

main_services.name: Understand how criminal charges are assessed and prosecuted

supporting.name: Learn how a criminal charge is assessed and prosecuted
supporting.desc: The BC Prosecution Service explains the four phases of a criminal case: investigation, charge assessment, prosecution and appeals, and the charge assessment standard Crown Counsel apply.
supporting.users: Accused persons, victims, members of the public

legislation.full_name: Crown Counsel Act  -  Charge assessment and conduct of prosecutions

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: British Columbians can access justice and have their human rights protected
goals.description: The ministry protects human rights under the Human Rights Code and funds legal aid and free legal help so people can resolve legal problems regardless of income.

main_services.name: Get help with a human rights complaint or protection

supporting.name: File a human rights complaint
supporting.desc: The independent Human Rights Tribunal mediates and adjudicates complaints of discrimination and harassment under the B.C. Human Rights Code.
supporting.users: People who believe they've experienced discrimination or harassment

legislation.full_name: Human Rights Code  -  Complaints and Human Rights Tribunal

supporting.name: Certify a guide dog or service dog team
supporting.desc: Guide and service dog handlers can apply for certification, which increases public safety, raises training standards, and improves public access and tenancy protections for certified teams.
supporting.users: People with disabilities who use a guide or service dog

channels.label: Guide Dog and Service Dog Certification

legislation.full_name: Guide Dog and Service Dog Act  -  Certification of dog and handler teams

supporting.name: Get information about your rights under the Human Rights Code
supporting.desc: Information about the B.C. Human Rights Code is available in multiple languages, explaining protections from discrimination and harassment in employment, housing, and services.
supporting.users: General public

legislation.full_name: Human Rights Code  -  Protected characteristics and prohibited discrimination

main_services.name: Get free or low-cost legal help

supporting.name: Apply for legal aid
supporting.desc: Legal Aid BC provides free legal help for eligible B.C. residents with criminal, family and immigration law matters, funded by the Ministry of Attorney General.
supporting.users: People who cannot afford a lawyer

legislation.full_name: Legal Services Society Act  -  Provision of legal aid

supporting.name: Get free self-help legal information at a Justice Access Centre
supporting.desc: Justice Access Centres provide self-help resource rooms, legal information, needs assessment, and referrals for family and civil legal issues, in person, by phone or by videoconference.
supporting.users: People with a family or civil legal issue

supporting.name: Get a referral to a lawyer
supporting.desc: The BC Legal Referral Service (formerly the Lawyer Referral Service) connects people with a lawyer for a free initial consultation.
supporting.users: General public

supporting.name: Get free legal help through a pro bono program
supporting.desc: Access Pro Bono connects eligible people who cannot afford a lawyer with free legal help and volunteer lawyers across B.C.
supporting.users: People who cannot afford legal representation

foundational_services.name: Provide legal advice and representation to government
foundational_services.desc: The Legal Services Branch provides legal advice, drafts legislation, and represents government ministries and agencies in litigation.

foundational_services.name: Conduct criminal prosecutions independent of political direction
foundational_services.desc: The BC Prosecution Service makes charge assessment decisions and conducts prosecutions and appeals independently of police and government, guided by the Crown Counsel Act.

legislation.full_name: Crown Counsel Act  -  Independence of Crown Counsel

foundational_services.name: Coordinate justice system reform through the Justice and Public Safety Council
foundational_services.desc: The Justice and Public Safety Council develops a strategic vision and plan for the justice and public safety sector and advises the minister on reform priorities.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, justice system partners

legislation.full_name: Justice Reform and Transparency Act  -  Justice and Public Safety Council

cross_cutting: Grow the economy and create jobs
Reduce costs for families, including housing
